title: Secure Boot or Image Hash Mismatch on Industrial Appliance

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CR 3.4.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 CR 3.4 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.39: Secure Boot or Image Hash Mismatch on Industrial Appliance.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.39' running on index=ot_edge sourcetype="edge:attest", archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: HEC from appliance vendor or Edge Hub
spl:
index=ot_edge sourcetype="edge:attest" earliest=-7d
| where result!="OK" OR match(_raw,"(?i)hash mismatch|secure boot fail")
| join type=left device_id [ search index=ot_hist sourcetype="hist:alarm" earliest=-7d | stats count as alarms by device_id ]
| table _time, device_id, result, alarms
description: Correlates component attestation failures with subsequent process alarms to prioritize firmware integrity incidents.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_edge, ot_hist and sourcetypes edge:attest, hist:alarm. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Collect TPM/UEFI events from industrial gateways; (2) isolate device on mismatch; (3) compare to gold hash registry; (4) vendor RMA workflow. Indexes required: `index=ot_edge`, `index=ot_hist`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=edge:attest`, `sourcetype=hist:alarm`.
visualization: Table, Timeline, Single value (failed devices). Key fields: `]`, `alarms`, `device_id`, `result`.
knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning. PAM sessions from authorized engineering access, vendor remote support, or scheduled maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: HEC from appliance vendor or Edge Hub.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_edge` `sourcetype="edge:attest"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Collect TPM/UEFI events from industrial gateways; (2) isolate device on mismatch; (3) compare to gold hash registry; (4) vendor RMA workflow.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_edge sourcetype="edge:attest" earliest=-7d
| where result!="OK" OR match(_raw,"(?i)hash mismatch|secure boot fail")
| join type=left device_id [ search index=ot_hist sourcetype="hist:alarm" earliest=-7d | stats count as alarms by device_id ]
| table _time, device_id, result, alarms
```

Understanding this SPL

**Secure Boot or Image Hash Mismatch on Industrial Appliance (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 3.4)** — Correlates component attestation failures with subsequent process alarms to prioritize firmware integrity incidents.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_edge` `sourcetype="edge:attest"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HEC from appliance vendor or Edge Hub. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_edge; **sourcetype**: edge:attest. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_edge, sourcetype="edge:attest", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where result!="OK" OR match(_raw,"(?i)hash mismatch|secure boot fail")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.

Output fields: _time, ], alarms, device_id, result.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT engineering record and session recording the control owner would use in an investigation. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Timeline, Single value (failed devices).
grandmaExplanation: We connect component attestation failures with subsequent process alarms to prioritize firmware integrity incidents. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by user, object, result | sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Change data model (Change.All_Changes).
